title: Surprising fact to be revealed about Cruel Angels Thesis
display_title: Surprising fact to be revealed about Cruel Angels Thesis
wikitext:
[]: Surprising fact to be revealed about Cruel Angel's Thesis             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Sep 10, 2015 12:30 pm Hmm, this could be interesting for sure! tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Sep 10, 2015 4:43 pm Leaving us in suspense about Song-lyrics. I take it back, Anno isn't a troll, everyone else at Khara is.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 10, 2015 5:23 pm The secret is that if you sing the lyrics in the dark 3 times while looking into a candle-lit mirror, Yui appears behind you. I know this. I've tried it. All kidding aside, I wonder what it is they could have possible done to that sing that we haven't already picked up through various re-recordings, different vocal talents singing it (the VAs for Misato, Rei, and Asuka, all sang it in one album), or that music video Masayuki edited for it. I mean, the most obscure thing I know about the sing was that it had any male-choir sounds removed from it to make it sound "more maternal" with the female choirs, keeping in line with the thematics of the show itself. I wonder what else there is to reveal about it at this point, 20 whole years later.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Sep 10, 2015 5:32 pm It was actually written for a different show that rejected it?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 10, 2015 5:39 pm ^ Unlikely, since I believe that "Cruel Angel's Thesis" was the second attempt at writing an OP for Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Sep 10, 2015 5:39 pm The secret will be drowned out by background noise when she reveals it! Maybe it's something about the inspiration behind the song? I can't imagine it'll be a huge revelation, whatever it is. [/spoiler]

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Sep 10, 2015 5:51 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ^ Unlikely, since I believe that "Cruel Angel's Thesis" was the second attempt at writing an OP for Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 10, 2015 9:32 pm I think we're still waiting to find out just what the hell the female chorus is singing. Not telling us what the background chorus is saying is a chronic bad habit of Eva songs, come to think of it.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 10, 2015 9:41 pm ^ I thought someone had come to the conclusion that they might have been singing "love is sin" or something like that. (Though, now that I think of it, I don't remember where I read that piece of shaky trivia.)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 12, 2015 1:41 pm This One Weird Fact About Cruel Angel's Thesis Is Guaranteed To Surprise You!

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat Sep 12, 2015 3:21 pm The surprising fact is that she hasn't seen the show, which is not really surprising to me.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 12, 2015 8:02 pm NAveryW wrote: This One Weird Fact About Cruel Angel's Thesis Is Guaranteed To Surprise You!

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Sep 12, 2015 8:08 pm Wow, it's absolutely nothing. I'm more surprised that her ex-husband was dumb enough to waste so much money buying a cave in Turkey.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 12, 2015 9:14 pm Wait, did we get an actual update on this yet?

[]:             Quote Post by shinhito » Sun Sep 13, 2015 2:13 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Wait, did we get an actual update on this yet?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 13, 2015 9:52 am This kind of blows my mind. How can you make close to a million dollars off of a song for a show, but never say to yourself, "Man, this show is really popular, and made me a lot of money, I wonder what the fuss is about?".

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Sep 13, 2015 11:52 am The Eva Monkey wrote: This kind of blows my mind. How can you make close to a million dollars off of a song for a show, but never say to yourself, "Man, this show is really popular, and made me a lot of money, I wonder what the fuss is about?".

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Sep 13, 2015 2:17 pm drinian wrote: William Shatner has never watched most of Star Trek.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 13, 2015 2:32 pm I wonder if that cave is the reason he's her ex- husband.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Sep 25, 2015 5:17 pm Actors/singers enjoy acting and singing, but rarely spend time watching what they've done. They just move on to the next project. It's obsessive fans who watch shit over and over again.
